2015; (8) का.आ. 3319 (अ), तारीख 25 अ榉टूबर, 2016; और (9) का.आ. 21 (अ), तारीख 3 जनवरी, 2017 । -- 9 of 18 -- 10 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART II—SEC. 3(ii)] MINISTRY OF COMMERCE AND INDUSTRY (Department of Commerce) NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 4th June, 2018 S.O. 2288(E).—In exercise of the power conferred by sub-sections (3) and (5) of section 30 of the Tea Act, 1953 (29 of 1953), the Central Government hereby makes the following amendments further to amend the Tea (Marketing) Control Order, 2003, namely:- 1. Short title and commencement.- (1) This order may be called the Tea (Marketing) Control (Amendment) Order, 2018. (2) It shall come into force on the date of its publication in the Official Gazette. 2. In the Tea (Marketing) Control Order, 2003, (hereinafter referred as the principal Order), in paragraph 3A, in sub-paragraph (3), the following proviso shall be inserted, namely:- “Provided that an application made after ninety days shall be accepted provided sufficient reasons for delay are given.”. 3. In the principal Order, for paragraph 17, the following paragraph shall be substituted, namely:- “17. Fees – Every application for the issue of a licence or registration or renewal thereof shall be accompanied by fees as specified below:- (a) for the issue of registration of manufacturing unit - Rs. 1,20,000/- (b) for the issue of registration of buyer - Rs. 9,000/- (c) for the issue of fresh license of Auction Organizer - Rs. 2,00,000/- (d) for the issue of renewal of license of Auction Organiser - Rs. 1,25,000/- (e) for the issue of fresh license of Broker - Rs. 90,000/- (f) for the issue of renewal of license of Broker - Rs. 56,000/- : Provided that a manufacturer with more than one manufacturing unit shall submit separate application for registration for each manufacturing unit along with requisite application fee for each such application: Provided further that a buyer having more than one office or branch shall submit only one application indicating addresses of all its offices or branches along with the requisite application fee.”. 4.
